file.content:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

input.command: coop-send agent2 --type "SURVEY" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "Adding new 'ssn' type (Regex) placed immediately after the existing 'isbn' type definition at end of file"

coop-send agent2 --type "DRAFT" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --region "end of file, appended after isbn definition" --code "ssn = Regex(r\"[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{4}\")

hash_sha256 = Regex(r\"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}\")" --note "Merged tail: ssn added first (immediately after isbn), then hash_sha256 right after it. Both are standalone top-level Regex assignments, no shared lines otherwise."

content.content:
[
  {
    "from": "agent2",
    "to": "agent1",
    "content": "type: DRAFT
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
region: end-of-file additions after isbn
code: isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

# Matches a US Social Security Number (SSN) in the format XXX-XX-XXXX
ssn = Regex(r"\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}")

# Matches a SHA-256 hash: 64 hexadecimal characters
hash_sha256 = Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")
note: Proposing order: isbn (unchanged) -> ssn -> hash_sha256, each with a one-line comment. Adjust ssn comment/regex to your liking and reply with your own DRAFT if different; then we both AGREE on identical final text.",
    "timestamp": 1783541433.0821795,
    "timestamp_iso": "2026-07-08T20:10:33.082180",
    "fields": {
      "type": "DRAFT",
      "files": "outlines/types/__init__.py",
      "region": "end-of-file additions after isbn",
      "code": "isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

# Matches a US Social Security Number (SSN) in the format XXX-XX-XXXX
ssn = Regex(r"\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}")

# Matches a SHA-256 hash: 64 hexadecimal characters
hash_sha256 = Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")",
      "note": "Proposing order: isbn (unchanged) -> ssn -> hash_sha256, each with a one-line comment. Adjust ssn comment/regex to your liking and reply with your own DRAFT if different; then we both AGREE on identical final text."
    },
    "kind": "DRAFT"
  }
]

tool_use_result.originalFile:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

lines: +# Matches a US Social Security Number (SSN) in the format XXX-XX-XXXX
+# Matches a SHA-256 hash: 64 hexadecimal characters

Will write this verbatim after isbn's closing paren, separated by one blank line.",

      "note": "Agreed - adopting your exact text verbatim (no comments, ssn via [0-9] classes, hash_sha256 pattern [a-fA-F0-9]{64}). Will write this verbatim after isbn's closing paren, separated by one blank line."

]

Will write this verbatim after isbn's closing paren, separated by one blank line.",

    "timestamp_iso": "2026-07-08T20:10:41.152914",
    "fields": {
      "type": "AGREE",
      "files": "outlines/types/__init__.py",
      "region": "end of file, appended after isbn definition",
      "code": "ssn = Regex(r\"[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{4}\")\n\nhash_sha256 = Regex(r\"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}\")",
      "note": "Agreed - adopting your exact text verbatim (no comments, ssn via [0-9] classes, hash_sha256 pattern [a-fA-F0-9]{64}). Will write this verbatim after isbn's closing paren, separated by one blank line."
    },
    "kind": "AGREE"
  }
]

content.text: Detected a message race — we each AGREEd to the other's earlier draft, producing two different final texts. Need to reconcile before finishing.

input.command:
coop-send agent2 --type "DRAFT" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --region "end of file, appended after isbn definition" --code "isbn = Regex(
    r\"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}\$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}\$|97[89][0-9]{10}\$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}\$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]\"
)

# Matches a US Social Security Number (SSN) in the format XXX-XX-XXXX
ssn = Regex(r\"\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\")

# Matches a SHA-256 hash: 64 hexadecimal characters
hash_sha256 = Regex(r\"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}\")" --note "Our AGREEs crossed (race) - you agreed to my earlier no-comment draft, I agreed to your comment+isbn-included draft. Reconciling: this is the FINAL version (your commented draft), already written to disk on my end. Please AGREE to THIS exact text so we converge."

note: Confirmed final - updated my file to this exact text (comments included, ssn via \d). Converged.",

)
